stem: An engineer takes 10 measurements of body weight using a bathroom scale. The measurements have a mean of 199.1 kg and a standard deviation of 10.3 kg. However, calibration tests reveal that a bathroom scale systematically overestimates the true value by between 4.4% and 7.7%. Estimate the true body weight and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 199.1 kg. After correcting for the 6.1% bias: ≈ 187.7 kg. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (4.4%-7.7%) and sampling variability: [178.4, 197.2]. Point estimate: 187.7 kg.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
